question: Two farmers each have a watermelon patch of equal size. Farmer A's patch is divided into 20 equal rows, and Farmer B's patch is divided into 16 equal rows. If Farmer A harvests 14 rows of his watermelon patch, what is the greatest number of whole rows that Farmer B can harvest from his patch without harvesting a greater percentage of a patch than Farmer A harvested?

solution.strategy: Farmer A: 14/20 = 70%. Farmer B: greatest whole rows so rows/16 ≤ 0.70. 11/16 = 68.75%, 12/16 = 75% > 70%. So 11.
